name: Washing Soda (Sodium Carbonate)
description: A strong alkaline salt (pH ~11) that saponifies grease on contact, softens hard water, and boosts surfactant performance.
appearance: White crystalline powder or granules; odorless
solubility: 215 g/L in water at 20°C; insoluble in ethanol
chemistry: Strong alkaline (pH ~11). Saponifies grease — turns fat into soap. Softens water. Boosts surfactant action, denatures protein stains.
warnings: Wear gloves. Can fade colors, damage delicate fabrics. Don't mix with acids.
